text.title: Runbook as Markdown with Tagged Steps Core Rules
text.summary: 9 testable rules with rationale + source for inc-runbook-as-markdown-tagged-steps. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every actionable step MUST carry one of: `[read]`, `[write]`, `[approval-required]`, `[verify]`, `[wait]`. Tag-less steps are commentary, not actions.
rule.rationale: Reduces ambiguity for both humans and agents; tag absence means 'context, do not execute'.

rule.statement: `[read]` steps execute without approval token; outputs are captured to audit log.
rule.rationale: Read-only operations are safe by definition; gating slows incident response.

rule.statement: `[write]` steps MUST require a signed approval token (chain to `gov-approval-token-signed-jwt`); `[approval-required]` is a stronger label meaning 'human must explicitly approve at step boundary'.
rule.rationale: Capability gating + human-in-the-loop on dangerous steps.

rule.statement: `[verify]` steps MUST contain an executable assertion (a script invocation or a dashboard metric check); free-form 'check it looks ok' rejected.
rule.rationale: Verification without assertion is performance; assertions catch silent regressions.

rule.statement: Runbook parser MUST be pinned to a version; rule changes go through code review.
rule.rationale: Parser drift silently changes execution; lock and review.

rule.statement: Each step MUST carry a stable `id=<slug>`; agents reference steps by id, not line number.
rule.rationale: Line numbers shift; ids are stable across edits.

rule.statement: Runbook executor MUST support `--dry-run` that prints step plan + would-be tokens without executing writes.
rule.rationale: Dry-run is the only way to review a runbook plan before live execution.

rule.statement: Every step execution MUST emit an audit entry with step id, tag, outcome, timestamp.
rule.rationale: Without audit, post-incident review cannot reconstruct what the agent actually did.

rule.statement: If team has no runbooks or already uses a fully machine-driven tool, return `skipped`.
